title: Anti-insect material
abstract: The invention relates to a material that can be used in the manufacture of air-permeable anti-insect clothing, its elements or blankets. The proposed material comprises at least two layers: the first and the second layer, which are formed from a material that is unpierceable and unbiteable through by insect jaws or a thorn, wherein said first layer comprises interconnected (preferably in a chess-board order) convexo-concave surface segments with spaces between them, and the second layer contains interconnected surface segments with spaces between them. In addition, the surface segments of the first layer with their convex sides are outwardly directed. The surface segments in each layer are interconnected so as to permit air movement between them, and the surface segments of the second layer are arranged offset so as to cover the spaces between the surface segments of the first layer; wherein the first and the second layers are interconnected so as to permit air movement between the layers preventing the access of the insect body parts to the surface protected by the material.
claims: Claims Claims 1. An anti-insect material, comprising two layers: a first and a second layer made of a material that cannot be bitten through or stung though by insect jaws or sting, characterized in that the first layer comprises interconnected, convexo-concave surface segments with spaces between them, and the second layer comprises interconnected surface segments with spaces between them, wherein the first layer's convexo-concave surface segments with its convex sides are outwardly directed; wherein the surface segments in each layer are interconnected so as to allow air to move between them, and the second layer segments are arranged offset so as to cover the spacing between the convexo- concave surface segments of the first layer; wherein the first and the second layers are interconnected so as to allow the air to move between the layers preventing the access of the insect body parts to the surface protected by the material. 2. The anti-insect material according to the claim 1, characterized in that the surface segments of the first and the second layers are interconnected in a substantially chess-board order. 3. The anti-insect material according to the claim 1 or 2, characterized in that the surface segments of the second layer also have a convexo-concave shape; wherein convexo-concave surface segments of the first and the second layers with its convex sides are oppositely directed outwards. 4. The anti-insect material according to any one of the preceding claims, characterized in that said convexo-concave surface segments are essentially sphere segments, ellipsoid segments, cylinder segments, truncated cone segments, or polyhedron segments. 5. The anti-insect material according to any one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the convexo-concave surface segments of the first and the second layer are of the same size, and each spacing between the surface segments of each layer is smaller than the area of the surface segment. 6. The anti-insect material according to any one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the spacing between the surface segments of the first and second layer in the narrowest place is not less than 0.1 mm. 7. The anti-insect material according to any one of the preceding claims, characterized in that it further comprises a third air permeable layer placed above the first or below the second layer. 8. The anti-insect material according to any one of the claims 1 to 6, characterized in that it further comprises a third air permeable layer placed above the first layer and a fourth air permeable layer placed below the second layer. 9. The anti-insect material according to any one of the preceding claims, characterized in that its one or more layers are further provided with bands or threads designed to form a netting structure. 10. The anti-insect material according to the claim 7 or 8, wherein said third and/or fourth air permeable layers are made of fabric containing natural fibers. 11. A clothing, a part or element thereof, comprising an anti-insect material according to any one of the preceding claims.
